title: There's Naethin Like The Honest Nappy!
content:
There's naethin like the honest nappy!
        Whaur'll ye e'er see men sae happy,
        Or women, sonsie, saft an' sappy,
            'Tween morn an' morn
        As them wha like to taste the drappie
            In glass or horn?

        I've seen me daezt upon a time;
        I scarce could wink or see a styme;
        Just ae hauf muchkin does me prime,
            Ought less is little,
        Then back I rattle on the rhyme,
            As gleg's a whittle.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Burns delivers a powerful performance in "There's Naethin Like The Honest Nappy!"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
